title: A Robust and Explainable Machine Learning Framework for Early Lung Cancer Risk Prediction using Synthetic Data Augmentation and Cross-Dataset Validation
authors: B Rajani, K Varsha, K Srinivas, N Sivamani, K Tanveer Ahmed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Early detection of lung cancer significantly improves patient survival rates; however, machine learning models developed for early lung cancer risk prediction often suffer from limited generalization and lack of interpretability due to small, imbalanced, and institution-specific datasets. This study proposes a robust and explainable machine learning framework that integrates synthetic data augmentation and cross-dataset validation to enhance predictive performance and transparency. To address class imbalance and data scarcity, a generative adversarial network-based tabular data synthesizer (CTGAN) is employed to generate high-quality synthetic samples while preserving statistical properties of the original dataset. Multiple machine learning classifiers, including Random Forest, XGBoost, and Logistic Regression, are trained on both original and augmented datasets. Model robustness is evaluated using cross-dataset validation to assess generalization capability across heterogeneous data distributions. To ensure clinical transparency, explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) techniques such as SHAP are applied to quantify feature importance and provide case-level explanations. Performance is assessed using accuracy, AUC-ROC, precision-recall metrics, calibration curves, and generalization gap analysis. Experimental results demonstrate that synthetic augmentation improves minority class detection and reduces overfitting, while explainability analysis enhances trustworthiness and interpretability of predictions. The proposed framework contributes a systematic approach to improving reliability and transparency in early lung cancer risk prediction, making it applicable to clinical decision support systems in reality.

title: Thermal Management of EV Battery Pack by Detection of Thermal Runaway using Deep Neural Network
authors: Murali Dharan S, Sunil Raja R, Logapragash C, Gopikrishnan A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The growing adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) has highlighted the critical need for effective thermal management systems to overcome the risks of thermal runaway (TR) in lithium-ion battery packs, which can lead to catastrophic failures and reduced battery performance. This study proposes an integrated framework for the early detection of thermal runaway event and proactive thermal management in EV batteries using deep learning. Thermal images of the battery pack are augmented and sent from a Raspberry Pi to a U-Net convolutional neural network (CNN) for precise semantic segmentation of anomalous heat patterns. The U-Net architecture, with its encoder-decoder structure and skip connections, enables pixel-level identification of hotspot regions. When temperature exceeds a defined threshold (>50°C), the system activates cooling mechanisms to dissipate excess heat. In cases of confirmed thermal runaway conditions, MOSFET-based circuits provide rapid electrical isolation of affected cells. Experimental evaluation on a prototyped battery setup demonstrates improved thermal stability, reduced incidence of thermal runaway propagation, and enhanced battery pack longevity. This approach strengthens EV battery safety, supports energy efficiency, and to the development of intelligent, scalable thermal management solutions for next-generation sustainable mobility.

title: MOH-Transformer: track association with hierarchical attention
authors: Jie Shen, Kaicheng Fu, Jinli Meng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Track association aims to determine whether two interrupted tracks originate from the same target, which is a core challenge in multi-target tracking. Conventional approaches mainly rely on artificially designed features or simple similarity metrics. They struggle to model complex spatio-temporal dependencies and deep associations between track segments. This paper proposes a hierarchical attention-based model for interrupted track association. The model includes two trajectory encoders and one fusion encoder. Each trajectory encoder processes the point sequence and statistical features of one track segment independently. The fusion encoder integrates the learned representations for binary association decision. Each trajectory encoder projects point-wise features and global trajectory features into the same latent space, concatenating them into sequences before feeding them into a Transformer. The self-attention mechanism captures local motion patterns and global motion dependencies simultaneously. Experiments are carried out on the MTAD datasets with complex trajectory scenarios. Results demonstrate that this method outperforms traditional methods and existing neural network models. It achieves an association accuracy of 95%. This proves its effectiveness for interrupted track continuation.

title: Construction of Detailed Equivalent Circuits for Turbogenerators Based on Standstill Frequency Response Analysis
authors: Farid Leguebedj
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: This paper introduces an analytical approach for estimating the time constants of a synchronous machine based on Stand Still Frequency Response (SSFR) testing. The proposed method offers significant advantages over the traditional approach, which relies on curve fitting to represent the operational inductance as a function of frequency, often leading to imprecise and non-unique results. The analytical technique utilizes linear system theory to identify poles and zeros in the frequency response, determining the optimal order of the equivalent circuit to accurately model the machine. The method is straightforward, feasible, and highly effective, but it requires an optimization process.

title: A Deep Learning–based Predictive GPS Safety Monitoring System With Minimal Network Dependence
authors: S. Aadhitya, K. Mohan, T.R. Manikandan, S. Mohammed Noorul Aslam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Reliable personal safety monitoring remains challenging in environments with unstable or limited internet connectivity. Existing safety systems rely heavily on continuous data transmission to remote servers, resulting in delayed alerts, increased battery consumption, and reduced operational reliability in low-signal regions. Reactive alert mechanisms further limit preventive capability, as notifications are often generated only after boundary violations occur. High false alarm rates caused by static threshold-based monitoring reduce user trust and increase alert fatigue. In addition, excessive network synchronization exposes sensitive location information, raising privacy concerns. This work presents a predictive GPS-based safety monitoring framework designed to operate with minimal network dependence while ensuring proactive anomaly detection and secure alert communication. A 100-meter geofence boundary is established around monitored locations, with early warning alerts triggered when movement approaches within 10 meters of the predefined perimeter. Sequential spatio-temporal mobility data are processed using a Long Short-Term Memory network to model behavioral patterns and compute a dynamic safety score. The predictive module enables early identification of abnormal trajectory deviations prior to boundary breach. Offline mapping support and local geofence evaluation reduce bandwidth consumption and ensure uninterrupted functionality without continuous connectivity. Secure in-application notifications transmit alerts to linked contacts with controlled data synchronization. The proposed system demonstrates improved detection reliability, reduced false alarms, optimized battery usage, and enhanced privacy protection. Local processing combined with predictive intelligence establishes an efficient, scalable, and context-aware safety monitoring solution suitable for deployment in low-connectivity environments.

title: VGG16-based Transfer Learning for Multi-Class Kidney CT Image Classification
authors: Shankar M, Anjali S, Manikandan R, K.Asritha, Shaik Heena Sireen, Sekh Mohammad Alamin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Kidney abnormalities such as cysts, tumors, and kidney stones are commonly evaluated using computed tomography (CT), but manual review of large image volumes is time-consuming and may introduce inter-observer variability. To address this problem, this study presents a transfer-learning framework for multi-class kidney CT image classification using the VGG16 architecture. The model was developed on a publicly available dataset of 12,446 CT slices grouped into four classes: Normal, Cyst, Tumor, and Stone. The workflow combines image resizing, normalization, Gaussian filtering, and controlled training-time augmentation to improve image quality and support model generalization. A stratified 70%/15%/15% split was used for training, validation, and testing. On the held-out test set, the proposed VGG16-based model achieved 92% accuracy, 0.93 macro-precision, 0.91 macro-recall, and 0.92 macro-F1 score, outperforming the shallow CNN and ResNet-50 baselines under the same experimental settings. These findings indicate that transfer learning can provide a practical and computationally efficient solution for automated kidney CT screening and may support radiologists by improving diagnostic consistency and workflow efficiency.

title: IoT based Groundnut Storage & Drying Automation System with Rotating Tray
authors: Kamali B, Kathiravan M, Mariselvam G, Murugan A, Petchithai V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This research project is to develop and design an IoT-based groundnut drying and storage monitoring and control system. Groundnuts are highly sensitive to temperature and humidity variations, and poor post-harvest handling generally leads to fungal growth, aflatoxin infestation, and huge losses in money. The presented system overcomes these issues by continuously sensing environmental conditions like temperature and humidity with the help of smart sensors and automatically running fans and heaters to maintain the optimal environment. This renders the process less manual but predictable, effective, and consistent. The system also uses a servo motor–operated rotating tray unit that facilitates uniform drying of groundnuts on both sides of the tray in order to prevent non-uniform moisture distribution. The system integrates local display of data for real-time feedback and cloud storage of data to provide remote access and instant notification, allowing farmers to make informed decisions even away from the storage facility. The project emphasizes affordability, scalability, and usability for small- and medium-scale farmers. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system effectively maintains optimal storage temperature and humidity, reduces spoilage risk, and improves energy efficiency compared to conventional storage methods.

title: Thermodynamically Constrained Variational Quantum Graph Learning for Near-Term Noisy Quantum Processors
authors: Ayush Mahanta, Jasmeet Kaur, Kapil Kumar, Manav Jain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Quantum machine learning has demonstrated a promising theory, but there are still few applications on Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) processors because of decoherence and noise build-up as well as optimization instability challenges. Current quantum graph learning studies tend to make assumptions about ideal quantum operations that are inapplicable in realistic conditions of real hardware. This paper suggests a variational quantum graph learning model with a thermodynamic constraint that incorporates entropy-focused regularization and circuit architecture that makes use of hardware considerations. The decrease in entropy production varies because the optimization of the proposed approach controls it and thereby affects barren plateau effects and enhances gradient stability without rising circuit complexity. Experiments were performed on a simulation level with realistic quantum noise models, that is, depolarizing and amplitude damping channels. The findings portray a better convergence stability, less entropy growth and more noise resistance compared to the standard variational quantum learning approaches. The study has identified the significance of thermodynamic constraints in quantum models of learning, and give a viable route to the sustainable and scalable graph-based quantum learning of near-term quantum computers.

title: An Area and Power-Optimised Multiplier-Less MAC Unit for Artix-7 FPGA-based Signal Processing
authors: S.Girish Babu, S. Rambabu, B. Raghavendra, A.B.S Meghasyamachari, D.Murthujavali, K.Kulasekhar Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This paper presents a resource- and power-efficient multiplier-less multiply–accumulate (MAC) unit for FPGA-based applications. The proposed architecture eliminates the conventional hardware multiplier by employing a shift-and-add computation approach along with an optimized accumulator to reduce switching activity and hardware complexity. The design is described in Verilog HDL and synthesized and simulated using the Xilinx Vivado Design Suite targeting an Artix-7 FPGA. Experimental results show that the proposed MAC unit achieves a 15.5% reduction in LUT utilization and a 26.7% reduction in flip-flop usage compared to the conventional fixed-precision MAC architecture. A significant 75% reduction in DSP slice utilization is obtained, along with reductions of 43.75% in static power, 14.5% in dynamic power, and 14.8% in total on-chip power consumption. These results confirm that the proposed architecture provides improved area and power efficiency, making it highly suitable for low-power and resource-constrained FPGA-based digital signal processing applications.

title: Tunicate Swarm Optimization for High-Dimensional Parameter Tuning of an 8-DOF Electric Vehicle Model
authors: Soniya Patil, Rajeev R. Pathak, Leah S. Joshi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: High-fidelity electric vehicle (EV) dynamics models are essential for the design and validation of advanced control and safety systems, where inaccurate parameterization can significantly degrade predictive accuracy. This work presents an automated optimization-based framework for tuning 26 coupled vehicle and tire parameters of an eight-degree-of-freedom (8-DOF) EV model using Bayesian Optimization (BO) and Tunicate Swarm Optimization (TSO). The calibration objective is formulated as a weighted normalized root mean square error between a tunable vehicle model and a validated reference model, evaluated across multiple dynamic driving maneuvers. Both optimization methods are executed under identical parameter bounds and evaluation budgets to ensure a fair comparison. Quantitative results demonstrate that TSO consistently achieves lower final cost values and improved tracking accuracy, particularly in maneuvers involving strong nonlinear and coupled dynamics, while BO exhibits faster initial convergence due to surrogate-assisted search. The study concludes that population-based optimization provides superior robustness for high-dimensional EV model calibration, whereas surrogate-based methods are effective for rapid coarse tuning under limited evaluation budgets.

title: Token-Anchored Semantic Drift: Untargeted Transferable Attacks Against Commercial MLLMs
authors: Tianying Shen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Commercial multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are increasingly deployed through closed APIs, yet their robustness to untargeted visual attacks remains underexplored. Existing transfer-based perturbations often only degrade image quality, leading the model to respond that the input is "blurry" or "unclear" rather than producing a concrete semantic error. Our key observation is that an effective untargeted attack against commercial MLLMs must simultaneously induce global semantic drift and preserve enough intermediate structure to remain visually coherent to the victim. Based on this observation, we propose TASD (Token-Anchored Semantic Drift), which couples image-level semantic drift over an ensemble of surrogate vision encoders with token-level anchoring on projected intermediate hidden states under stochastic transformed views. We further provide a conditional transferability analysis for why ensemble optimization, transformed-view training, and token anchoring can improve black-box transfer, and we establish a caption-drift evaluation protocol tailored to commercial MLLMs. Experiments on five commercial MLLMs show that TASD reaches 45.4% to 49.7% ASR under the default ϵ = 16 setting, outperforming the strongest transfer baseline by 21.7 to 25.7 points; multi-surrogate optimization further raises GPT-4o ASR to 68.7%, and larger perturbation budgets push ASR to 87.4% to 88.5%.

title: AI-Driven Predictive Healthcare Analytics for Early Disease Detection and Clinical Decision Support
authors: Abdul Aziz
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This study addresses the growing need for proactive healthcare by leveraging artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) for early disease detection and enhanced clinical decision support. Traditional diagnostic approaches are often reactive, identifying diseases only after symptoms appear, which limits treatment effectiveness. To overcome this limitation, the proposed framework integrates heterogeneous healthcare data—including electronic health records, medical imaging, genomic data, and real-time patient monitoring—into a unified predictive analytics pipeline. The methodology employs an ensemble learning approach combining Random Forest, XGBoost, and Deep Neural Networks to improve predictive performance and robustness. Experimental evaluation across cardiovascular diseases, oncology, and diabetes demonstrates superior performance, achieving 94.78% accuracy and an AUC-ROC of 0.951, significantly outperforming conventional models. Additionally, SHAP-based interpretability enhances transparency and clinician trust in AI-driven predictions. The study also highlights key challenges such as data heterogeneity, privacy concerns, and algorithmic bias, while proposing strategies for ethical and scalable deployment. Overall, the proposed system enables a shift from reactive to proactive, precision-based healthcare, improving early diagnosis, optimizing treatment decisions, and enhancing patient outcomes.

title: Adaptive Prompt Generation Agent: A Dynamic Prompt Engineering Framework for Chinese Text Generation
authors: Tian Xie, Shuai Wu, Dong Zhang, Yifei Wang, Shukai Duan, Lidan Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: In the process of generating text by large language models, prompts play a crucial role. Existing methods suffer from limitations such as low efficiency in manual writing, rigid templates, and insufficient automation capabilities. To address these issues, this paper proposes an Adaptive Prompt Generation framework, APG-Agent, which establishes a closed-loop process encompassing task difficulty assessment, adaptive prompt generation, and large model response. Experiments demonstrate that on the LCSTS v2, THUCNews 2025, and FinCopy2025 datasets, APG-Agent achieves a 7.2% higher ROUGE-1 score compared to StructInstruct, CoPrompt, and AutoPrompt-Zero, with a 12.5% improvement in business compliance and an 18.7% reduction in label consumption. The APG-Agent framework provides a lightweight and automated solution that can be easily deployed in various Chinese industrial application scenarios.

title: An Ensemble Machine Learning Approach for Heart Diseases Prediction
authors: M Suresh, Umamageshwari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Millions of deaths worldwide are attributed to cardiovascular diseases each year, making them a major global health concern. The growing demand for early diagnosis, along with the limited availability of cardiology specialists, has increased the need for automated screening systems in healthcare. In this work, a machine learning–based method is proposed to predict heart disease at an early stage using clinical attributes. The Heart Failure Clinical Records dataset is used to analyze the performance of four machine learning models: Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Random Forest. To improve the reliability of the models, preprocessing steps such as normalization, missing value handling, and exploratory data analysis are performed. The effectiveness of each model is evaluated using metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and the area under the Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curve (AUC).The experimental findings reveal that the Random Forest model outperforms the other approaches, achieving an accuracy of 83.33% and an AUC of 0.879. These results demonstrate the strength of ensemble learning techniques and support their use in clinical decision support systems for the early detection of heart disease.

title: An Efficient Encrypted Graph Query Scheme Based on Bloom Filters
authors: Linbo Zhou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of cloud computing, an increasing number of users outsource graph-structured data to cloud servers. How to efficiently query encrypted graph data with well-defined and controlled information leakage has become a pressing problem. Existing searchable symmetric encryption (SSE) schemes are primarily designed for the keyword-document inverted index model and cannot be directly applied to common neighbor queries on graph data. Moreover, most dynamic schemes fail to simultaneously achieve forward security and query efficiency. To address these shortcomings, this paper proposes an efficient encrypted graph query scheme based on Bloom filters. The proposed scheme transforms graph edges into an encrypted inverted structure indexed by nodes, employs pseudo-random permutations (PRP) for reversible encryption of neighbor nodes, and leverages cross-tags together with Bloom filters for efficient server-side common neighbor filtering. Compared with existing approaches, our scheme maintains forward security while reducing per-record storage overhead by over 50%, and the search complexity is linear only in the neighbor count of the query node with the smallest degree.

title: A Lightweight Artificial Intelligence Method for Official Document Process Automation
authors: Rongsong Li, Wen Gu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The intelligent routing of official documents is a critical component of modern Office Automation (OA) systems. However, existing works often struggle with multi-source heterogeneous document formats, oversimplified routing topologies, and suboptimal feature utilization. Furthermore, the deployment of large-scale transformer models introduces prohibitive computational costs. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a lightweight, artificial intelligence-based method for smart official document dispatching. Our approach introduces a decoupled prediction mechanism that first forecasts the next workflow node and organizational duty, which is subsequently mapped to specific personnel, thereby ensuring robustness against staff turnover. We implement a comprehensive data preprocessing pipeline leveraging optical character recognition (OCR) and format conversion to handle diverse file types. By concatenating Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) text representations with encoded categorical data, the method optimally captures both semantic and structural information. Then, nine classical machine learning models including K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Random Forest (RF) are utilized for prediction. Extensive experiments on real-world corporate datasets demonstrate that our lightweight method, which operates efficiently on standard CPUs, achieves robust performance while maintaining low deployment complexity.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Driven Energy-Efficient Routing Protocol with Dynamic Power Control and Load-Aware Multipath Optimization for MANET
authors: Mamoun Yaqoub Mimoun Alhammali, Wilson Jeberson
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Mobile Ad Hoc Networks (MANETs) have become critical communication models of infrastructure-less networks like disaster recovery, military operations, and Internet of Things (IoT) applications. Nevertheless, the current routing protocols are usually characterized by limitations such as low power efficiency, deterministic power assignment, and high frequency route failures that have adverse impacts on network performance and network life. To solve these issues, the current paper will present a Graph Attention Network (based) Dueling Double Deep Q-Network (GAT-D3QN) routing framework to find a solution to energy-efficient and adaptive routing in MANETs. Graph Attention Network learns spatial links between adjacent nodes and scales the significance of links between them with the help of attention mechanisms, which allows topology consciously extract features. The Dueling Double Deep Q-Network helps make intelligent routing choices through enhancing learning stability and minimizing overestimation bias. Moreover, a dynamic transmission power control scheme is used to adjust transmission levels according to the quality of links and node distance, and a load-conscious multipath routing policy is used to allocate traffic to multiple paths in order to decrease the path congestion. The simulations show that the protocol proposal has a 93.6 percent packet delivery ratio, 96 ms end-to-end delay, 59.8 J energy usage and 835 seconds network lifetime which is better than the performance of AODV, DSR, and DQN-Routing protocols.

title: A Multimodal Emotion Recommendation System using Text and Facial Expression Analysis
authors: Harshini Sri D, Jeeva Santra G, Ashok Kumar K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Emotions are very important for human preference and decision-making. This proposed work uses a Multimodal Emotion Recommendation System that makes the use of both text information and face expressions for effective emotion detection of the user. The use of a BERT-based deep learning technique for text-based emotion analysis. Furthermore, face-based emotion analysis would be accomplished with the help of a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). Hence, with the help of both information, it would overcome any drawbacks or inefficiencies of a single modal-based emotion analysis system, ultimately refining the efficiency of recommendation systems. This would further enable the end-user to provide relevant information, like movies or products that match the mood of the user. Simulation outcomes eventually reveals that multimodal information would increase effective emotional analysis, ultimately resulting in more meaningful recommendations.

title: 1:2 Efficient Demultiplexer Design using QCA Technology
authors: Shivam Kumar, Vijay Kumar Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: In modern nano-electronic devices, the size of electronic circuits is reduced at nanoscale which reduces the circuit complexity and increases speed, but there are many major challenges to design ultra nano-electronic digital circuits. Quantum-dot Cellular Automata (QCA) is an ensuing nanotechnology that can be used instead of traditional Complementary Metal Oxide Semiconductor (CMOS) because QCA works on binary data instead of current flow. In this current research paper, a lesser QCA cells, ultra-low power consumption and area-efficient 1:2 demultiplexer design is proposed based on QCA technology. For the proposed design, 20 QCA cells are utilized, which use QCA majority gate and inverter to control the output signal. Layout of the design using QCA designer tool, which simulate the design and analyze the cell count, area, cost, delay of proposed 1:2 demultiplexer. These features make it suitable for use in future ultra-low power nano-electronic devices.

title: An Efficient and Secure Encrypted Multi-Keyword Search Scheme
authors: Tuoda Pan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: For cloud storage scenarios, multi-keyword searchable encryption needs to balance query efficiency and update security while ensuring data confidentiality. Existing schemes for multi-keyword queries often face challenges such as high server-side computational overhead, linkability of historical tokens after updates, and complex engineering implementation. This paper proposes an encrypted multi-keyword search scheme that combines a counter-based index and a Bloom filter(add-only). The scheme reduces the candidate set size by generating search address sets based on the least frequent keyword. It accelerates intersection determination via a Bloom filter for rapid membership checks, reducing the cost of server-side exact matching. Forward security is achieved through keyword counters and address re-randomization for document additions. Implementation-level optimizations are applied to the Bloom filter structure and hashing process. Experiments comparing the proposed scheme with the VFSSE scheme on a constructed real-world dataset demonstrate that our scheme achieves better query latency performance in multi-keyword scenarios while maintaining low storage overhead and high accuracy (with a configurable false positive rate of 0.01%).

title: Stochastic Dynamics of Human-Bot Interactions on Social Platforms: A Three-Population SDE Model
authors: Zihan Sha, Wenhao Cui
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Social bots now make up 9-15% of active Twitter accounts, with recent advances in Large Language Models enabling a new generation of sophisticated "sleeper" bots that convincingly mimic human behavior and evade traditional detection systems. (The revised Abstract highlights the latest research background: LLM-driven Bot threats.) But most research still treats detection as a yes/no problem. By the time you decide an account is a bot, it's already done its damage. What we don't understand is dynamics: how do humans, bots, and detectors actually co-evolve?This paper models them as three populations competing in a stochastic system. Humans leave when bots harass them. Attackers constantly inject new bots. Detectors learn from interactions but decay without upkeep. The math is Lotka-Volterra with noise—multiplicative Itô processes, because fluctuations scale with population size.I calibrate the model using real proportions from TwiBot-22: 860,057 humans, 139,943 bots. Then I run 1000 Monte Carlo simulations.Three things hold up even when parameters shift by 20%. First, platforms have an 11% chance of losing a third of their human users within a month—but outcomes vary wildly. Same parameters, different futures. Second, detector quality matters more than attack volume. A better detector beats fighting every bot. Third, aggressive detectors backfire. Catch too many bots and you scare off humans. The ones that balance bot suppression with human retention end up with 22% more people.

title: Bita: A Conversational Assistant for Fairness Testing
authors: Keeryn Johnson, Cleyton Magalhães, Ronnie de Souza Santos
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 7th International Workshop on Bots and Agents in Software Engineering
abstract: None

title: Beamforming Reflective Metasurfaces Supporting Polarization Control
authors: Tianke Qiu, George V. Eleftheriades
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Reflective electromagnetic metasurfaces (MTSs) provide a passive platform for manipulating the reflection of incident electromagnetic waves, offering opportunities for beamforming and polarization control in wireless communication systems. However, systematic 3D design methods that account for anisotropic unit cells and full-wave interactions remain limited. In this paper, a 3D full-wave design procedure for reflective MTSs based on the integral equation method and tensor impedance boundary conditions is presented. The MTS consists of impedance sheets placed on top of a grounded dielectric slab. Based on a method of moments formulation that explicitly incorporates coupling between unit cells, the impedance values are optimized via gradient descent to achieve beamforming and polarization control of the reflected field. A numerical example demonstrates the beamforming capability of the proposed method, where precise amplitude and phase control through auxiliary surface waves allows the synthesis of a target sector pattern. For practical implementation, a regression neural network is employed to establish an inverse mapping between the optimized impedance tensors and the geometric parameters of anisotropic unit cells featuring three degrees of freedom, enabling efficient realization of spatially varying anisotropic impedance distributions. Two MTSs are designed and experimentally validated at 10 GHz based on an introduced novel physical unit cell. The first, with 576 unit cells spanning four wavelengths by four wavelengths, performs anomalous reflection and converts linear to right-hand circular polarization (RHCP), achieving 13.0 dB cross-polar discrimination and 21.8 dB directivity, with an instantaneous 3-dB fractional gain bandwidth of 6.2%. The second, with 1296 unit cells, splits the reflected wave into RHCP and LHCP beams in different directions, with measured directivities of 23.9 dB and 23.7 dB, respectively, and a bandwidth of 7.6%. These high directivities are achieved by accounting for mutual coupling and by exploiting auxiliary surface waves that redistribute incident power across the MTS.

title: Sensitivity Analysis for Antenna Devices through Interval Arithmetic - A Generalized Approach
authors: Nicola Anselmi, Paolo Rocca, Andrea Massa
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a novel method for the sensitivity analysis of electromagnetic (EM) systems whose transfer function (TF), that is the input-output (I/O) relationship between the input parameters affected by tolerance and the system response (i.e., the arising EM performance of interest), is not available in closed (or explicit) form. The method is a generalized analytic technique based on the Interval Analysis (IA). First, an analytic surrogate model (SM) of the TF is defined by means of a learning-by-example (LBE) approach starting from a set of available I/O examples. Then, the LBE-derived SM is extended to intervals through IA to yield inclusive, yet finite, performance bounds of the output system response when the control parameters are affected by unknown, but bounded, tolerances. A set of representative numerical examples is reported to validate the proposed IA-LBE method as well as to assess its effectiveness and reliability when dealing with realistic EM systems (e.g., antennas) for which the TF is not explicitly known.

title: Measuring Linear Array Mutual Coupling Terms and Element Positions from Embedded Element Patterns
authors: Jacki Gilmore, Michele Pugno, Annie Cuyt
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a method to derive the antenna element positions and S-parameters of linear antenna arrays of identical elements from measurements of the embedded element patterns. The problem is formulated as an exponential data modelling problem, and validated exponential analysis is used to extract the element positions and the S-parameters. Results in the calculation of the element positions indicate combined absolute-relative errors (i.e. absolute error divided by 1+ the exact element position) of less that 0.1% for a synthetic example of an 11-element array, and less than 0.2% for a 4-element experimental example. The combined absolute-relative error in the calculation of the coefficients from which the S-parameters are derived is < 0.002 for the synthetic example. There is excellent agreement between S-parameters directly measured with a VNA and S-parameters determined using the proposed method.

title: Manufacturing and testing of electrospun nanofiber-enhanced mask textiles
authors: Réka Alexandra Pózmán, László Székely, Dr. Richárd Cselkó, Mohd Yasir Al Saif, Balázs Illés, Attila Géczy
abstract: Electrospun nanofibers offer a promising route for enhancing the filtration performance of textile-based masks, particularly in situations where certified respirators are scarce. This study presents a simplified and economical electrospinning device capable of producing uniform mask-sized nanofiber layers without the need for a syringe pump. Electric-field simulations were used to optimize key design parameters, including the number and spacing of needles, revealing that a two-needle configuration provides the most uniform deposition while minimizing mutual field distortion. Experimental tests further identified the optimal solution properties, electrode distance, and voltage required to generate defect-free nanofibers. The resulting electrospun PA-6 layers were characterized by scanning electron microscopy and demonstrated significantly improved filtration performance when applied to cotton substrates. These findings establish a practical, low-resource approach for producing nanofiber-enhanced mask materials suitable for emergency deployment.

title: RUBCA: Detecting Anxiety Levels in Arabic Tweets Using a Rule Based Classification Approach
authors: Eithar I. Almujalli, Muna M. Alhammad
abstract: Anxiety detection within social media platforms has become an essential focus in computational mental health due to the prevalence of dialectal ambiguity and orthographic inconsistencies in user-generated Arabic content. Existing models often exhibit limited interpretability and reduced sensitivity when applied to linguistically diverse Gulf regions. This study aims to develop a robust, explainable system for identifying anxiety-related patterns in Arabic tweets.We present RUBCA, a hybrid symbolic–distributional framework integrating three core modules: a manually constructed Arabic anxiety lexicon aligned with eight clinically validated symptom dimensions; a symbolic classification engine employing nested conditional rules for dialectal variance; and an iterative human-in-the-loop refinement strategy for lexicon and rule tuning. While the inference stage is fully deterministic and explainable, the lexicon refinement stage incorporates distributional embeddings to capture semantic proximity across dialectal variants. The architecture incorporates co-occurrence verification, binary encoding of symptom presence, and multi-label assignment to capture complex psychological constructs. Semantic perturbation functions and context-aware filters further enhance resilience against orthographic noise. Simulation results demonstrate RUBCA’s superiority over eleven state-of-the-art models, achieving 93.7% accuracy, 91.4% precision, and 95.2% category-level coverage across Gulf datasets.

title: DriveAware: Multimodal Wearable Sensing–Based Machine Learning Approaches to Detect Driver Distraction
authors: Heba Montaser, Ghazal Bargshady, Hakki Gokalp Ustun, Girija Chetty, Houshyar Asadi, Jeroen Van Boxtel
abstract: Driver distraction is a major public health and road safety concern, linked to delayed reactions, impaired attention, and increased crash risk. This study introduces a simulation-based framework to detect distraction by systematically collecting multimodal physiological signals, electrodermal activity (EDA) and photoplethysmography (PPG), across three driver states (baseline, undistracted, and distracted) under diverse road conditions. Sessions and secondary-task orders were randomised, and performance was assessed using nine-fold cross-validation by leaving five subjects out each fold for evaluation. Features were extracted from both modalities and refined using Shapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) to identify the most significant features, enhancing model transparency and offering new insights into physiological markers of distraction. This design frames the method as an enhanced AI decision-support pipeline suitable for driver distraction alerting. We conducted a comprehensive evaluation of multiple machine learning models, comparing unimodal (PPG-only, EDA-only) and multimodal fusion approaches. In particular, middle fusion with Random Forest and XGBoost, and late fusion through soft voting and stacking, were systematically explored. The results show that late fusion through soft voting and stacking, combined with XGBoost and SHAP-based feature selection, significantly outperforms single-modality approaches, achieving accuracy of (73.37 ± 3.52)% for three level of baseline, undistracted, and distracted driving. Multimodal gains were consistent across folds, underscoring the reliability benefits of decision-level fusion over single-modality models. Whereas, we obtained the best performance in XGBoost by (71.19 ± 2.85)% for single modality of EDA only and by (69.63 ± 4.07)% for single modality of PPG only. These findings highlight the potential of interpretable multimodal physiological monitoring, combined with wearable sensing, as a promising direction for real-time driver-state monitoring, subject to further validation.

title: Enhancing Multimodal Recommender System Performance Using Large Language Models
authors: Soohyun Woo, Seonu Park, Xinzhe Li, Seongkook Lee, Jaekyeong Kim
abstract: With the rapid advancement of e-commerce platforms and the overwhelming amount of information available online, recommender systems have emerged as essential tools for helping users efficiently discover items aligned with their preferences. Online reviews, in particular, offer rich information for understanding both user preferences and item features, and have been widely utilized in existing multimodal recommender systems. However, most prior approaches have not sufficiently addressed modality-specific noise in multimodal reviews, thereby constraining their capacity to precisely represent users’ experiences. Furthermore, by merely extracting and fusing features from texts and images without considering the structural heterogeneity between modalities, these approaches often fall short in accurately predicting user preferences. To address these limitations, this study proposes multimodal aspect summarization for recommender systems (MAS-Rec), a novel model that leverages large language models (LLMs) to summarize multimodal reviews by aspect, thereby enhancing preference prediction. The proposed model is structured as follows: First, LLMs are employed to independently summarize user preferences and item features into aspects from textual and visual data, respectively. Second, these aspect-based summarizations are then encoded using a bidirectional encoder representations from transformers (BERT) to extract semantic features. Finally, a co-attention mechanism is applied to model the semantic interactions between user preferences and item features. Experimental results on two review datasets provided by Amazon.com demonstrate that MAS-Rec outperforms existing unimodal and multimodal models. These findings demonstrate that summarizing images by aspect using the LLM effectively captures the semantic connections between texts and images, thereby enabling more accurate prediction of user preferences compared to traditional approaches.

title: Graph Neural Network-Based Regional Analysis of EEG During Imagined Swallowing: Identifying Cortical Contributions
authors: Sevgi Gökçe Aslan, Bülent Ylmaz
abstract: Imagined swallowing (MI-SW) is a promising paradigm for brain–computer interface (BCI)-assisted dysphagia rehabilitation; however, reliable EEG-based decoding remains challenging because of the low signal-to-noise ratio and high inter-subject variability. In addition, prior EEG-based MI-SW studies have not explicitly modeled interregional brain interactions and have rarely employed subject-independent validation frameworks, limiting their generalizability. To address these limitations, this study proposes a regional graph-based EEG decoding framework that integrates (i) region-wise graph modeling of EEG channels, (ii) leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) subject-independent validation, and (iii) occlusion-based interpretability analysis. EEG data were collected from 30 healthy participants under two paradigms: volitional imagined swallowing and sensory-induced imagined swallowing, which was achieved by holding water in the mouth before imagery. Spectral, event-related desynchronization, entropy-based, and nonlinear features were extracted and classified using Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN). The classification task was formulated as a binary problem distinguishing Rest versus Imagined Swallowing conditions. All experiments were evaluated using leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) cross-validation to assess subject-independent decoding performance. The proposed framework achieved robust subject-independent performance across regions, with the sensory-induced paradigm consistently outperforming volitional conditions. Peak classification accuracy of >87% was obtained in the premotor region under sensory-induced conditions, whereas the volitional paradigm showed optimal performance using whole-head electrode integration. No direct comparison with conventional CNN or CSP-based baselines was performed in this study; therefore, performance gains are reported within the proposed framework under a consistent evaluation setting. Occlusion-based interpretability analysis further confirmed physiologically meaningful patterns, with low-frequency (δ–θ) desynchronization and entropy/complexity features emerging as the dominant discriminative markers of imagined swallowing. These findings demonstrate that integrating spatial graph representations with the peripheral somatosensory context enhances the EEG-based decoding of swallowing motor imagery and supports the development of reliable and interpretable BCI systems for dysphagia rehabilitation.

title: Simulation-Based Evaluation of Underwater Sensor Network Performance Using DBR and Broadcast MAC in Shallow and Deep Sea Scenarios
authors: Koko Oktavianus Asmara, Wirawan, Endang Widjiati
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: Reliable and adaptive underwater monitoring systems are essential due to the diverse oceanographic conditions in the Southeast Asian maritime region, encompassing both shallow coastal areas and deep-sea zones. Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks (UWSNs) enable realtime data acquisition in such environments. This study employs the Aqua-Sim-NG simulator on the NS-3 platform to evaluate the performance of Depth-Based Routing (DBR) combined with Broadcast MAC under shallow- and deep-sea scenarios. Simulations are conducted at two acoustic frequencies (3 kHz and $\mathbf{2 5 ~ k H z}$) across four network topologies-combinations of one or two sink nodes with 5 or 10 sensor nodes. Underwater acoustic properties, including the Sound Speed Profile (SSP) and SOFAR channel, are considered alongside key performance metrics such as end-to-end delay, throughput, and packet loss ratio. Results show that $\mathbf{2 5 ~ k H z}$ yields lower latency and higher throughput in shallow marine environments, whereas 3 kHz enables more reliable long-distance communication in deep-sea conditions with significantly reduced packet loss. The simulation results align well with theoretical predictions from the Thorp attenuation model, indicating minimal deviation within numerical precision. These findings highlight the importance of network topology and frequency selection in UWSN deployment. The study provides practical insights for sensor placement strategies in Indonesian waters, where bathymetric diversity significantly affects network performance. Future work will consider the impact of various underwater noise sources, including ambient, shipping, and thermal noise, on communication performance. In addition, the Bit Error Rate (BER) will be evaluated alongside existing performance metrics-delay, throughput, and packet loss-to provide a more comprehensive assessment of acoustic channel quality.

title: Detecting Data Poisoning Attack in Federated Learning Healthcare System
authors: Saadhana Santoshi J, Rajeswari D
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is an application that allows training models collaboratively, retaining the privacy of clients, but being extremely susceptible to attackers that poison data, especially in healthcare settings with heterogeneous and sensitive data. Such attacks impair the model integrity and lower the predictive performance and jeopardize patient safety. In order to solve this dilemma, we introduce an innovative adaptive defense model, which integrates anomaly recognition, secure aggregation, and powerful filtering of clients to detect and avoid malicious updates in the training process. The algorithm also uses homomorphic encryption and similarity-based clustering to identify forged or poisonous gradient updates with uncompromising privacy conditions. Wide-ranging experiments on real-world medical data proves the fact that the given method is highly effective to counter adversarial attacks, with the final global model accuracy reaching 99.96, surpassing the solutions currently available, including Trimmed-Mean and Bulyan. The present paper is a contribution to a scalable, secure, and privacy-preserving answer to the resilient FL in the healthcare application.

title: Enhanced AI-based Biometric Authentication using Recurrent Neural Network
authors: Gopinath P, Gopi Krishnan P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This research presents an enhanced biometric authentication system combining fingerprint and iris recognition using Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN). The system incorporates advanced pre-processing techniques, such as Gaussian filtering, histogram equalization, and Canny edge detection, to enhance input data quality. Sequential dependencies in biometric features are captured using Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) networks and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), enabling robust feature extraction and classification. A dual-stream RNN architecture independently processes fingerprint and iris features, followed by feature fusion for final authentication. The proposed framework addresses challenges such as intra-class variability, partial occlusion, and environmental variations, achieving over 97% accuracy on public datasets. Designed for real-time applications, this system ensures high reliability and efficiency for secure access control in critical domains.

title: Edge-Enabled Autonomous Drone Navigation for IoT Data Gathering: A Hybrid Deep RL-GNN Framework with Energy-Aware Path Planning
authors: Mukkawar Sowmya, Swarna Kamalam Vaddi, D Devender, Mallareddy Adudhodla, Pravallika Koppula, Pavan Kumar Reddy Manellore
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Efficient data collection in expansive Internet of Things (IoT) networks remains challenging due to spatially distributed sensors and stringent energy constraints. This paper presents a hybrid Deep Reinforcement Learning–Graph Neural Network (DRL–GNN) framework for autonomous drone navigation, coupled with energy-aware path planning to optimize IoT data gathering. At the edge tier, federated multi-agent Deep Q-Networks learn adaptive flight policies that maximize coverage and minimize age of information (AoI), while a GNN module captures sensor topology to refine decision making. Path planning incorporates battery state estimation and wind disturbance models to ensure mission feasibility. Simulation results in Gazebo/ns-3 demonstrate a 42% increase in coverage and a 35% reduction in energy consumption over heuristic baselines, with packet delivery ratio exceeding 99%. Field trials in urban, agricultural, and forest environments validate robustness and scalability. This integrated approach advances autonomous UAV deployment for real-time IoT sensing in dynamic scenarios.Keywords— Unmanned Aerial Vehicles, Deep Reinforcement Learning, Data Collection Optimization, Wireless Sensor Networks, Trajectory Planning, Q-Learning, Network Scalability

title: Dynamic Energy-Aware Power Allocation for Life-time Maximization in UAV Networks
authors: Yang Yu, Yinchao Ge, Jia Chen, Benjun Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: This paper maximizes the operational lifetime of a static unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) cluster providing downlink coverage. We formulate a Dynamic Weighted Sum Power Minimization (DWSPM) problem, where a real-time weight, inversely proportional to each UAV’s remaining energy, balances intra-cluster energy consumption. A Dynamic Energy-aware Power Allocation (DEPA) algorithm is then proposed to solve this problem. Simulations show that DEPA outperforms benchmark schemes, including traditional energy-efficiency maximization and fixed-weight strategies, achieving the longest system lifetime especially under high user density. This validates that explicitly optimizing for longevity via real-time energy awareness is paramount for sustainable UAV-assisted communication.

title: A Cost-Effective Portable Ventilator with AMBU Bag Mechanism and Real-Time Leakage Detection using LCD Display
authors: P.Gopinath, V. Dharshini, M. Hariprakash, S.V. Barath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The accessibility of low-cost and reliable respiratory support remains a significant challenge in critical healthcare, particularly in low-resource environments. This paper provides a portable and cost effective ventilator system which is designed to help breathing using an AMBU bag procedure. This ventilator system utilizes an embedded system to control breathing cycles while constantly monitoring emergency breathing parameters. The LCD display is used to alert caretakers whenever unusual conditions are identified, which improves patient safety. The proposed system was estimated through hardware, which confirms constant oxygen delivery and reduced power usage. Performance evaluation specifies high precision in observing pressure, airflow, leakage conditions. Due to its low cost, compactibility and reliable monitoring properties, the improved ventilator is well suited for critical response units, rural healthcare centers, and ambulances where the usage of traditional ventilators is limited. The hardware results show 96% accuracy in measuring airflow, pressure and leakage detection in the oxygen supply tube.

title: Deep Learning-Based Signal Disentanglement for Surface Acoustic Wave Passive Wireless Sensing Under Strong Interference
authors: Meixin Chen, Hao Jin, Ao Ming, Xiaoliang Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Passive wireless Surface Acoustic Wave (SAW) sensing systems are widely deployed in harsh industrial environments, yet their reliability is often compromised by intense electromagnetic interference (EMI) and environmental noise. This paper proposes an AI-driven signal disentanglement framework that integrates a 1D-CNN with an input-level attention mechanism to enhance signal integrity. The proposed attention module adaptively re-weights digitized sequences to highlight segments with high signal-to-noise (SNR) ratios, suppressing background noise before hierarchical feature extraction. To ensure robustness against transient impulse interference, we implement a mode-based decision fusion logic that aggregates multiple sampling iterations during frequency sweeps. Experimental results demonstrate that the framework accurately identifies valid SAW echo responses even at SNR levels below 5 dB. Notably, the system successfully locks onto the true resonance frequency (e.g., 415.6 MHz) despite overlapping high-amplitude EMI. Compared to traditional methods like ICA and GA-BPNN, this approach offers superior deterministic classification and real-time performance for industrial monitoring.

title: Adaptive Frequency-Guided Fusion Mamba with Dynamic Cross-Modal Attention for Robust Infrared-Visible Object Detection
authors: Qi Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Cross-modality object detection using paired infrared (IR) and visible (RGB) images has attracted significant attention due to the complementary information carried by these two modalities. Mamba-based methods employing selective state space models (SSMs) have recently shown strong capability in building hidden interaction spaces for cross-modal feature fusion, achieving linear computational complexity while capturing long-range dependencies. However, existing Mamba-based fusion approaches still suffer from two critical limitations: they fuse features uniformly across all frequency components, ignoring that low-frequency semantic content and high-frequency structural details exhibit fundamentally different modality-specific noise patterns and therefore require distinct fusion strategies; and their cross-modal channel interaction is applied statically, without adapting to the actual illumination conditions of each scene, which causes misaligned channel weighting between daytime and nighttime imagery. In this paper, we propose an Adaptive Frequency-Guided Fusion Mamba (AFG-FusionMamba) framework that addresses both limitations. We design an Adaptive Frequency Decomposition Module (AFDM) that separates cross-modal features into low- and high-frequency sub-bands using learnable wavelet-inspired filters with orthogonality regularization to prevent filter collapse, and applies modality-symmetric fusion operators to each band. We introduce a Dynamic Illumination-Aware Channel Attention (DIACA) module that estimates a per-scene illumination coefficient from the RGB stream and uses it to dynamically reweight cross-modal channel interactions in a fully differentiable manner, with an IR-anchored fallback mechanism that ensures graceful degradation when the RGB stream is severely corrupted. We further present a Dual-Stream Frequency-Aware State Space (DSFSS) block that augments the hidden state space transitions with a phase-preserving spectral magnitude alignment operator, enforcing cross-modal frequency consistency. A frequency consistency regularization loss is additionally incorporated into the total training objective to promote spectral alignment across detection scales. Extensive experiments on LLVIP, M<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>FD, and FLIR-Aligned datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance, with gains of up to 2.3% mAP on M<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>FD and 3.1% mAP on FLIR-Aligned over the Mamba-based baseline, while maintaining competitive inference speed.

title: Mapping Forest Fire Risks Using Deep Learning: A Case Study in An Giang Province, Vietnam
authors: Muhammad Suliman, Ramon Antonio Rodriges Zalipynis
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: With the escalating frequency and intensity of global wildfires, there is an urgent need for predictive models that balance accuracy with operational practicality. Building upon the TFDeepNN wildfire prediction framework of Truong et al. [1], this study develops a deep learning framework for wildfire risk assessment in Vietnam’s An Giang province, focusing on early detection of significant fire events. Building upon the TensorFlow-based deep neural network approach previously applied in Vietnam, we implement deployment-oriented improvements including dynamic fire growth metrics, higher-temporal-resolution MERRA-2 weather data integration, and improved JAXA LULC classifications. Our enhanced model achieves 91.4% overall accuracy with 74.8% recall for significant fires and 92.5% precision, while maintaining 97.7% specificity and only a 2.3% false alarm rate. At the same time, a recall of 74.8% means that 25.2% of significant fires are missed, so the model is best used as a decision-support layer alongside existing monitoring and verification procedures rather than as a standalone alarm source. Through feature importance analysis using SHAP, we identify fire spread rate as the dominant predictor (37.6% importance), supporting the physical plausibility of the model’s decisions. Overall, the contribution is best described as applied integration and optimization of multi-source data and deployment-aware evaluation, rather than a new deep learning method.

title: Enhancing Fetal Health Prediction Through Feature Reduction and Ensemble Learning
authors: Thripthi P Balakrishnan, Mahesh Bathula, Keerthi Addluri, Likhitha Modem, Mahendra Bala
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Perinatal complications remain a notable cause of neonatal morbidity and mortality, making accurate fetal health monitoring a critical clinical priority. Cardiotocography (CTG) is the standard non-invasive tool for fetal surveillance; however, human interpretation is highly subjective and prone to inter-observer variability, necessitating automated prediction systems. This study suggests a robust and interpretable machine learning model for three-class fetal health classification using CTG data. The proposed work combines SMOTE for class-imbalance correction, PCA for feature importance analysis reducing twenty-one attributes to ten clinically significant ones, and ensemble learning for reliable classification. Six classifiers — Logistic Regression, Naive Bayes, Random Forest, Multilayer Perceptron, CatBoost, and Stacking — along with a soft-voting ensemble are measured. CatBoost and the soft-voting ensemble achieve a peak test accuracy of 97%, while stratified five-fold cross-validation yields a mean accuracy of 95.12% with a standard deviation of 0.24%, which confirms that model is stable. The trained model is integrated in a Flask-based web-based application for real-time clinical prediction. The proposed framework combines class balancing, feature interpretability, ensemble robustness, and real-time deployment within a single pipeline, addressing gaps that existing works have only partially resolved.

title: Performance Optimization of Intel Multi-Core CPUs using Memory-Aware Data Parallelism
authors: Arunkumar Velayutham, S.Muthumarilakshmi, J.Jayasudha, R. Jagadeesh Kannan, R Babuji, S Sujatha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Modern high-performance computing applications require efficient utilization of multi-core processors while addressing memory bottlenecks and cache contention. This study proposes a memory-aware data parallelism framework to improve performance scalability and energy efficiency in Intel multi-core CPU architectures. The approach integrates workload profiling, cache-aware data partitioning, and bandwidth-aware thread scheduling to optimize data locality and minimize memory stall cycles. Experimental evaluation was conducted on an Intel Core i7-10700K (8C/16T) processor using a structured parallel workload. Performance metrics including runtime reduction, speedup, cache utilization, and memory bandwidth efficiency were measured. Energy consumption was also evaluated to analyze the performance– energy trade-off. The experimental analysis demonstrates that memory-aware partitioning significantly improves runtime behavior, cache efficiency, and energy consumption compared with conventional parallel execution strategies

title: Activity-Aware Power Management Architecture For FPGA-Based Edge System using On-Die Thermal Feedback
authors: Rukumani Devi D, Shanthi K G, Abishek S, Ashwin Balaaji J, Afeef A, Sam Kumar V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: In edge systems that use FPGA technology, thermal stress was created and resulted in excessive power consumption. The activity-aware power management architecture uses the on-die thermal feedback capabilities of the Artix-7 XADC, which provides data about absolute temperature measurement, rate-of-change detection, thermal classification, one-hot power FSM, and dynamic clock gating. The design was applied in the XC7A35T-FTG256 IC at running clock frequency of 50 MHz which reduce the junction temperature from 43.1°C to 37.7°C with the approximate total chip total power consumption of 0.104 W.

title: Towards Zero Backflow: A Low-Power Device for Automated IV Infusion Monitoring and Auto-Stop Mechanism
authors: Monisha R, Trishaa S G, Vaishali G, Sudha J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The most popular and widely used medication in hospitals is IV infusion System. However, we need a manual effort to monitor the flow of IV fluids and solutions, this can lead to mistakes. To halt the flow at the appropriate moment, the nurse manually verifies the closest stage of IV fluid completion. Blood will flow back into the IV tube if the IV bag is filled completely and the flow is not stopped in time. The reverse blood flow is caused by the pressure differences between the IV fluids and the blood. This leads to complications in the patient’s body and risk of infections. By automating the IV infusion process, monitoring and stopping the IV flow, this issue is fixed. This problem is resolved by automating the monitoring of IV infusion process and blocking the flow of IV fluids by using a clamp-based mechanism. This is developed as a device that can be connected to the IV stand, and the tube is set between it by the nurse. This detects the level of solution ,air bubbles, temperature of solution, and occlusion, and automatically stops the flow of solution in the tube by blocking the tube and preventing the reverse blood flow. By integrating this system with the mobile application, the nurses in the hospital will receive a notification regarding the completion of the solution. This solution is reliable because of its low cost and low power consumption. The device also uses a buzzer for sound indication and an LED light indicator to show the flow blockage. This device is easier to fix, handle, and works based on Wi-Fi with a battery and recharging capability.

title: Hybrid CNN-LSTM Edge Computing Framework for Intelligent Aquarium Health Management
authors: M. Jayanthi, A. Rini Infanceya, S. Sahithya, S. Sakthi Pavithra, J. Sanjana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Maintaining stable environmental conditions and ensuring early disease detection are critical challenges in aquarium management and small-scale aquaculture. Manual monitoring is often inconsistent, reactive, and unable to detect subtle environmental fluctuations or early-stage infections. This work proposes a hybrid Edge AI–based intelligent aquarium monitoring system that integrates deep learning–driven disease classification with predictive water quality analytics to enable autonomous and preventive management. A custom multimodal dataset was developed to train and evaluate the system under real operating conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed disease detection model achieved an accuracy of 94.2% with an F1-score of 0.936, significantly outperforming baseline convolutional models. For environmental prediction, the time-series forecasting model achieved an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.941 with low RMSE, enabling early identification of potential threshold violations approximately 12 minutes before critical levels were reached. The integration of predictive and reactive control reduced false corrective actions and improved system stability. Statistical evaluation confirms improved reliability, reduced manual intervention, and enhanced aquatic safety. The proposed system demonstrates the effectiveness of combining edge intelligence with environmental analytics, offering a scalable and cost-effective solution for intelligent aquarium management and small-scale aquaculture applications.
